forecast-period.text: For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.

A series of low pressure systems passing through the Bight will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend.
* Calls to 1300 number cost around 27.5c inc. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecast or Local Waters Forecast at http://www.bom.gov.au/sa/forecasts/map.shtml for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for these coastal zones.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots south of Port Noarlunga in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Adelaide Metropolitan Waters
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots tending northwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the middle of the day. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres around midday.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers.
West to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots tending south to southwesterly below 10 knots during the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the morning.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Variable about 10 knots.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Cloudy.
